title: Telnet and Auth modules missing or nonfunctional
author: jcarletto27
body:
Hi, and thanks for the great software!

I'm running into an issue where I cannot get Telnet or Auth functions to activate, no matter what features are active or inactive in the configs, so I'm hoping you can point me in the right direction. I see the ESP commands in the logs when compiling, so it seems like it's trying, but when I attempt to check or set the [ESP130] command I get an invalid command.

[compile_upload_log.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29625235/compile_upload_log.txt)

Boards tested : ESP32-C3-Zero, ESP32-S3 Super Mini
Compiled on Garuda Arch Linux, using version 3.3.4 of platform IO, and latest pull from repo

title: Broken Docs Link in README
author: Armaan-Raisinghani
body: Hi, it appears the link in README for docs (http://esp3d.io/esp3d/v3.x/index.html which redirects to https://esp3d.io/esp3d/version_3x/) returns a 404. The correct docs are on https://esp3d.io/esp3d/version-3x/ (hyphen, not underscore), perhaps there was a typo when setting up the redirect?

title: ESP3D shutdown when Macro Button used
author: celticbrown
body:
Made a macro button on ESP3D and the following 1 line code used..
M150 B255 R255 U255 P255
This turns on Neopixel light as expected But, it then shuts down the printer with a pop up message "shutting down" 
There is no M112 or M81 as I said in the 1 line code.

title: COMMUNICATION_PROTOCOL MKS_SERIAL Issue
author: Mistahbrock
body:
Hello Luc / World

I've been looping around with my ESP8266-12S MKS Module constantly resetting the par couple of day
today I ( using ChatGPT ) found the fix / hack

    /* Serial Communication protocol
     * RAW_SERIAL // Basic serial protocol, without data change
     * MKS_SERIAL // This is a MakerBase communication protocol, used with MKS
     * printers and TFT, it encapsulated data in a custom protocol
     */
    //#define COMMUNICATION_PROTOCOL RAW_SERIAL
    #define COMMUNICATION_PROTOCOL RAW_SERIAL

these line made the firmware stable and running, and as I'm by no means a skilled coder, I have no idea why it works, but it does : 
     // hack by Brock / ChatGPT :
    #undef MKS_SERIAL
    #define MKS_SERIAL 1

And by the way, thanks for a great piece of soft / firmware, I love it to the bones

Best regards, Brock :)

title: Missing License Information via GitHub API
author: LicenseProliferation
body: While retrieving license information through the GitHub REST API for compatibility analysis, we noticed that the repository currently does not return license information via the API: https://api.github.com/repos/luc-github/ESP3D ``` "license": null, ``` Because of this, our automated tools interpreted the project as having no license, which introduces compatibility risks against its upstream projects. However, after manual review, we found that this repository is actually licensed under the GPL. To ensure the license is correctly detected by the GitHub API and displayed consistently in the repository metadata, we suggest renaming the existing `gpl.txt` file in the root directory to `LICENSE`. This will allow both the API and the repository page to clearly reflect the license information. Current situation: Example metadata for a repository:

title: websocket connection error
author: joydeepb47
body:
Description

After opening the webUI, it closes automatically after 2-3 seconds showing a websocket connection error. The problem was reported and closed with a comment that it couldnot be duplicated in https://github.com/luc-github/ESP3D/issues/996 . But here this issue  is duplicated in my case without any possible fix after multiple failed attempts.. When using ESP3D (versions 3.0.0 and 3.0.1) on a NodeMCU (ESP8266) to bridge to an Arduino Mega 2560 running Grbl-Mega-5X, commands sent via the Web UI HTTP endpoint (/command?cmd=) return a generic "ESP3D says: command forwarded" message. However, the actual serial output from the target board (such as GRBL status or settings) is not returned in the HTTP response. Additionally, WebSocket connections required for real-time DRO updates consistently fail across both versions.

Expected Behavior

Sending a command like ? or $$ via the HTTP API should return the actual serial data from the Arduino Mega (e.g., <Idle|MPos:0,0,0...> or $0=10...) directly in the response body. The WebSocket connection should be stable to allow the WebUI to update coordinates in real-time.

Actual Behavior

HTTP API: Sending http://[IP]/command?cmd=? results in the string ESP3D says: command forwarded, but no GRBL status data is received by the browser.

WebSocket Failure: Connection attempts to ws://[IP]/ws or ws://[IP]:81 consistently result in a WebSocket connection failed error in the browser console. This issue is identical in both v3.0.0 and v3.0.1.

DRO Stagnation: Because the API does not return coordinates and the WebSocket fails, the WebUI remains "Disconnected" or fails to update position, even though the serial bridge is physically active via other ports (Telnet).

Code Observations (Command/Accept Handling)

The issue appears linked to how ESP3D v3 handles command synchronization compared to v2.x. Specifically:

In the current code, the command processing logic (related to the _accept or process function for incoming requests) immediately triggers a "forwarded" acknowledgement to the web client.

The logic within the available() check for the serial buffer appears to be bypassed or decoupled during the HTTP request lifecycle. While the command is successfully sent to the board, the return data is not being pulled from the serial stream back into the same HTTP session or WebSocket stream.

This asynchronous behavior in the _accept mechanism seems to prevent the firmware from waiting for the serial response before closing the HTTP output, effectively breaking the feedback loop for real-time GRBL monitoring.

This represents a significant change from the synchronous response pattern in v2.x.

Steps to Reproduce

Flash ESP3D v3.0.0 or v3.0.1 to a NodeMCU (ESP8266).

Connect to an Arduino Mega 2560 running Grbl-Mega-5X at 115200 Baud.

Access the official ESP3D WebUI (v3.0.0-alpha3).

Send any command via the terminal.

Observe the "forwarded" response in the logs and the "WebSocket connection failed" error in the browser inspector.

Hardware

ESP Board: NodeMCU v2 (ESP8266)

Flash Size: 4MB

Target Board: Arduino Mega 2560

Firmware on Target: Grbl-Mega-5X (4-axis foam cutter)

Firmware & WebUI Version

ESP3D Firmware: Tested on 3.0.0 and 3.0.1

ESP3D WebUI: 3.0.0-alpha3 (official release)

Communication Settings

Baud Rate: 115200

Mode: Bridge / Telnet Enabled

Additional Context

While the Telnet bridge (Port 23) functions correctly for external G-code senders, the integrated WebUI is non-functional for machine control because it cannot retrieve the serial stream or establish a WebSocket connection. For 4-axis Hotwire CNC applications, the lack of real-time position reporting via the WebUI makes it impossible to monitor tapered cuts or wire positioning safely.

title: No files displayed when looking at TFT SD
author: jimhiggs
body:
I have BTT TFT35 which is showing option to view and list from both onboard SD (BTT SKR PRO V1) and the TFT SD. 
But in ESP3D I have adjusted the settings to allow this but no files are listed when I chose TTF SD.
I have configured marlin(Latest) setting

#define SDCARD_CONNECTION ONBOARD 
Anything obvious I am missing?

title: Used Configurator and Compiles and updates ESP01S 4MB But no AP or Station wifi and cant communicate Serially
author: jimhiggs
body:
I am using a ESP01S $MB and programmer purchased from Amazon.
I Use Platformio for a lot of other projects so I disabled it in vscode and enabled Pioarduino.
I used the Configurator to create my Configuration.ini, platformio.ini and index.html.gz.
Building works without error and upload seems successful with result
Compressed 474640 bytes to 336951...
Writing at 0x00000000... (4 %)
.......
Writing at 0x00050000... (100 %)
Wrote 474640 bytes (336951 compressed) at 0x00000000 in 29.7 seconds (effective 
128.1 kbit/s)...
Hash of data verified.
If I then try to use serial monitor in vscode I get a few lines of garbage. Same if I try to connect using Putty.
Wifi AP is not available. I tried compiling with the Station SSID defined but that also did not work.

Also tried to upload filesystem image in Pioarduino. this failed with :
File  md5: b13656e68372eea15c1b8f734f0f44ff
Flash md5: 178231681a8d37a3b5faf524d3e0c060
MD5 of 0xFF is a5f6fbe334f9febc346340dcda56b51c

A fatal error occurred: MD5 of file does not match data in flash!

title: [Question] Should web view work using https?
author: vicitacal
body: My esp is connected through Keenetic domain system But when I try to log in to this domain, I am redirected to the HTTPS protocol. The frontend loads but there is a communication error with the board. The following error appears in the browser console: > Content: The page at 'https://printer.*****.keenetic.pro/' was loaded over HTTPS, but requested an insecure XMLHttpRequest endpoint 'http://printer.*****.keenetic.pro/command?cmd=....'. This request has been blocked; the content must be served over HTTPS. However, if you go to the address specified in the error message, the data will be successfully returned. Is this a bug and should the frontend be using a different protocol, or am I doing something wrong? ESP3D Firmware: [v3.0.0-beta1](https://github.com/luc-github/ESP3D/releases/tag/v3.0.0-beta1) ESP board: [ESP32](https://aliexpress.ru/item/1005005704190069.html?sku_id=12000034061352780) ESP3D-WebUI Version: [V3.0.0-beta1](https://github.com/luc-github/ESP3D-WEBUI/releases/tag/v3.0.0-beta1) Wifi mode: STA Flash method: PlatformIO Target Firmware: Name: Marlin Version: [Marlin 2.1.3 beta 3](https://github.com/MarlinFirmware/Marlin/releases/tag/2.1.3-b3) Board used (please complete the following information): Creality CR10 Ender 3 board

title: [BUG]Temperature and Position not Updating Despite Working Serial Comms
author: retromaster
body:
**Describe the bug**

Upon startup, the ESP3D web interface is stuck at "waiting for temperatures list". I am sure, however that the serial comms between the ESP module and the printer is fine because the printer is responding well to the commands I enter in the Terminal panel. For example, I enter M503 and I am able to get the whole settings report and there does not seem to be any data corruption. Even better, I send the M105 command and get back the temperature readings, at which stage the Temperature and Extruder panels update themselves with the data. However, they do not continue updating automatically. Another thing, the Settings > Marlin panel also seems to work fine.

**To Reproduce**

Issue manifests itself right at startup.

**Expected behavior**

I expect the web UI to display the temperatures and position on startup and continue updating them. 

**ESP3D Firmware:**

- ESP3D FW version: [3.0.0.8b1] (Downloaded latest branch as ZIP from github).
- ESP3D-WebUI Version: [3.0.0-4b1.M2.1] 
- Wifi mode: STA (Client) 
- Flash method: PlatformIO

**Target Firmware:**

Marlin 2.1.2.5 (with some modifications for Anycubic 4MAX Pro support).

**Board used **

ESP Board: NodeMCU v3 (ESP8266 w/ 4MB Flash).

**Additional context**

This may very well be some kind of configuration issue, it seems like something simple that I may have missed. If not, is there some kind of serial comm log accessible via the ESP3D-WebUI? Perhaps I could use that to diagnose the problem.

title: [Question]use of readData() on lua interpreter
author: kleffa
body:
local pin = readData([ESP201] P=4)
if pin == 1 then
    print("[ESP201] P=4 V=0 ")
else
    print("[ESP201] P=4 V=1 ")
end

I'm writing a couple of scripts like this one (just change state of flash on esp32cam). I know this can be just a digitalRead, but its about using readData() , this doesnt work, I missed something?
can readData used to read serial, like M27 data?

any help please to clarify it
